title: Code Indexing Engine
description: The system must index source code repositories using tree-sitter AST parsing to extract symbols (functions, classes, modules, files) and their relationships (calls, imports, contains).
acceptance_criteria: Repository clone and indexing completes without error;Symbols extracted for Go Python TypeScript Java Rust;Call graph relationships stored in SurrealDB;Re-indexing on git push updates only changed files

title: Multi-Language Support
description: The indexer must support Go, Python, TypeScript/JavaScript, Java, and Rust via tree-sitter grammars.
acceptance_criteria: Go files parsed with correct function and struct extraction;Python files parsed with class and function extraction;TypeScript files parsed with interface and function extraction;Java files parsed with class and method extraction;Rust files parsed with struct impl and function extraction

title: SurrealDB Graph Storage
description: All code metadata (symbols, relationships, requirements, links) must be stored in SurrealDB using a graph model with record IDs.
acceptance_criteria: Repositories stored with org ownership;Files linked to repositories;Symbols linked to files with line ranges;Relationships stored as graph edges;CBOR protocol used for Go SDK

title: GraphQL API
description: The system must expose a GraphQL API for querying repositories, symbols, requirements, and traceability links.
acceptance_criteria: Query repositories with nested files and symbols;Query requirements with linked code entities;Mutation to add repository by git URL;Mutation to import requirements from CSV or Markdown;Mutation to create and verify manual links

title: REST Health and Metrics
description: The API server must expose REST endpoints for health checks, readiness probes, and Prometheus metrics.
acceptance_criteria: GET /healthz returns 200 when server is running;GET /readyz checks SurrealDB connectivity and worker gRPC health;GET /metrics returns real Prometheus instrumentation data

title: JWT Authentication
description: Users must authenticate via JWT tokens with secure cookie-based sessions for the web UI and Bearer tokens for API clients.
acceptance_criteria: Login endpoint issues JWT with configurable expiry;Cookies are HttpOnly Secure SameSite Lax;Bearer token auth for CLI and extension;Admin password persisted to SurrealDB not lost on restart

title: CSRF Protection
description: All cookie-authenticated mutations must be protected against CSRF attacks via double-submit cookie pattern.
acceptance_criteria: Double-submit cookie pattern with X-CSRF-Token header;Bearer token API clients are exempt from CSRF;GET /api/v1/csrf-token returns token and sets sourcebridge_csrf cookie;GraphQL mutations from browser require valid CSRF token

title: Requirement Import from CSV
description: Users must be able to import requirements from CSV files with columns: id, title, description, priority, acceptance_criteria.
acceptance_criteria: CSV parsed with configurable column mapping;Rows with missing id or title are skipped;Acceptance criteria split on semicolons;Requirements stored in SurrealDB with source metadata;Deduplication by external ID on re-import

title: Requirement Import from Markdown
description: Users must be able to import requirements from structured Markdown documents with ## headings.
acceptance_criteria: Markdown headings parsed as requirement titles via regex;Body text parsed as descriptions;Priority extracted from bold metadata pattern;Acceptance criteria parsed from bullet lists;Requirements stored with source file reference

title: LLM-Powered Code Summarization
description: The system must generate natural language summaries of functions, files, and modules using an LLM provider.
acceptance_criteria: Summaries generated for individual functions with purpose inputs outputs dependencies risks;Summaries generated for files with function context;Summaries generated for modules with file context;Results cached by content hash to avoid redundant LLM calls;Circuit breaker prevents cascading failures on LLM timeout

title: Code Review Engine
description: The system must perform structured code reviews across five dimensions: Security, SOLID, Performance, Reliability, Maintainability.
acceptance_criteria: Review covers all five dimensions with specific findings;Each finding includes category severity message file_path start_line end_line and suggestion;Review results stored and retrievable via GraphQL;Reviews can be triggered from web UI and VS Code;Review score 0-10 returned per template

title: Code Discussion
description: Users must be able to ask contextual questions about selected code and receive AI-powered answers with references.
acceptance_criteria: User selects code and types a question;AI response includes answer text and code references;Related requirement IDs identified in response;Discussion accessible from web UI and VS Code;Discussion results include model and token usage

title: Code Explanation
description: The system must explain what a selected code entity does in plain language markdown.
acceptance_criteria: Explanation generated for any symbol in the index;Response is markdown describing purpose logic and edge cases;Results cached for repeat queries

title: Relationship Explanation
description: The system must explain the relationship between two code entities or between a requirement and code.
acceptance_criteria: ExplainRelationship RPC accepts two entity references;Response describes how the entities interact;Explanation available via GraphQL query

title: Semantic Requirement-to-Code Linking
description: The system must automatically link requirements to code entities using embedding-based semantic similarity.
acceptance_criteria: Embeddings generated for requirements and code symbols;Cosine similarity used to rank candidate matches;Confidence scores capped at 0.85 for semantic-only links;Links surfaced for human verification;Configurable similarity threshold with 0.6 default

title: Comment-Based Linking
description: The system must detect requirement references in code comments matching patterns like REQ-001 or ENG-42.
acceptance_criteria: Regex patterns detect UPPERCASE-DIGITS ID formats in comments;Links created with 0.95 confidence for exact matches;Comment links include file and line reference;Language-aware comment line detection for multiple syntaxes

title: Reference-Based Linking
description: The system must detect requirement references in commit messages, branch names, and changed code entities.
acceptance_criteria: Commit messages scanned for requirement ID patterns;Branch name scanned for requirement ID patterns;Links created at 0.7 confidence when entities provided;Placeholder links at 0.5 confidence when no entities specified

title: Test Correlation Linking
description: The system must correlate test functions with requirement IDs found in test names and docstrings.
acceptance_criteria: Test function names scanned for REQ-NNN and UPPERCASE-DIGITS patterns;TestReqNNN normalized to REQ-NNN format;Doc comments scanned for direct ID references;Links created with 0.85 confidence and TEST link type

title: Link Confidence Scoring and Multi-Signal Boost
description: All automated links must have confidence scores with multi-signal boosting when multiple link types agree.
acceptance_criteria: Confidence scores range from 0.0 to 1.0;Links deduplicated by requirement-entity pair keeping highest confidence;Multi-signal boost of 0.03 to 0.08 when 2 or more sources agree;Combined rationale shows all contributing sources;COMMENT plus SEMANTIC boost is 0.05

title: Human Link Verification
description: Users must be able to verify, reject, or manually create requirement-to-code links.
acceptance_criteria: Verify action marks link as human-confirmed with confidence set to 1.0;Reject action marks link as rejected and excluded from active view;Manual link creation specifies requirement and symbol with confidence 1.0;Verification publishes event via SSE;Verification status visible in traceability matrix

title: Batch Auto-Link
description: Users must be able to trigger batch auto-linking of all requirements to all code entities via autoLinkRequirements mutation.
acceptance_criteria: autoLinkRequirements processes each requirement against all symbols;Calls worker LinkRequirement RPC per requirement;Links above minConfidence threshold stored;Returns linksCreated count and requirementsProcessed count;Progress reported during long operations

title: Link Validation
description: The system must support validating whether an existing link is still valid by re-computing embedding similarity.
acceptance_criteria: ValidateLink RPC accepts a link and optional current symbol content;Embedding similarity recomputed between rationale and symbol;still_valid set true if similarity above 0.4;Confidence level enum returned based on new score

title: Link Quality Evaluation Framework
description: The system must support evaluating linking quality against a gold dataset with precision recall and F1 metrics.
acceptance_criteria: Gold dataset loaded from JSON with expected requirement-entity pairs;Predicted links compared against gold using requirement-entity key pairs;Precision recall and F1 computed;Confidence calibration evaluated at configurable threshold;True positive false positive and false negative lists returned

title: Web Dashboard
description: The web UI must show a dashboard with stat cards, platform stats, recent requirement links, and recent AI operations.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard shows stat cards for repositories requirements and coverage;Platform stats show files symbols links and total LLM tokens;Recent links table shows requirement symbol confidence and date;Recent AI operations table from LLM usage query;Empty state provides onboarding guidance

title: Repository List Page with Inline Forms
description: The web UI must show a list of repositories with inline Add Repository and Import Requirements forms.
acceptance_criteria: Repository list shows name status and file function class counts;Inline Add Repository form accepts name and path or URL;Inline Import Requirements form per repository accepts content and format;Status badges show indexing state;Last indexed timestamp displayed

title: Repository Detail Page with Six Tabs
description: The web UI must show a repository detail page with tabs for Files, Symbols, Requirements, Analysis, Knowledge, and Settings.
acceptance_criteria: Files tab shows all files with language and line count;Symbols tab has kind filter and name search with per-symbol Analyze button;Requirements tab has Auto-Link button and inline import form;Analysis tab has Discuss Code input and Review Code template selector;Knowledge tab shows artifacts with Generate buttons;Settings tab has Re-index and Remove Repository buttons

title: Requirement List Page with Split Pane
description: The web UI must show a requirement list with a split-pane detail view showing linked code symbols.
acceptance_criteria: Left pane shows all requirements with priority badges;Right pane shows linked code symbols with confidence badges when requirement selected;Requirement list shows ID title priority and link count

title: Requirement Detail Page with Manual Linking
description: The web UI must show a requirement detail page with description, linked code, manual link creation, and AI enrichment.
acceptance_criteria: Detail page shows full description tags and priority;Enrich with AI button calls enrichRequirement mutation;Linked code section shows symbols with verify and reject toggle;Symbol search form for creating manual links with rationale;Per-link confidence badge and verification status

title: Add Repository Form
description: The web UI must provide a form to add a new repository by name and git URL or local path.
acceptance_criteria: Form accepts repository name and path;Validation prevents duplicate repository paths;Indexing starts automatically after add via addRepository mutation;Progress indicator shows indexing status

title: Requirement Upload and Import
description: The web UI must provide inline forms for importing requirements from pasted CSV or Markdown content.
acceptance_criteria: Text area accepts pasted CSV or Markdown content;Format selector for markdown or csv;Import calls importRequirements mutation with repositoryId content and format;Results show imported count skipped count and warnings;Error reporting for malformed content

title: AI Analysis Triggers from Web
description: The web UI must allow users to trigger all AI analysis operations from the repository detail page.
acceptance_criteria: Analyze Symbol button per symbol calls analyzeSymbol mutation and shows inline results;Discuss Code text input calls discussCode mutation and shows answer;Review Code template selector calls reviewCode mutation and shows findings by severity;Auto-Link button calls autoLinkRequirements mutation with progress;Enrich Requirement button on requirement detail page

title: Onboarding Wizard
description: The web UI must guide first-time users through a three-step wizard: welcome, add repo, and done.
acceptance_criteria: Three-step wizard with progress bar at top;Step 1 shows welcome message;Step 2 prompts user to add a repository via addRepository mutation;Step 3 confirms setup is done;Accessible at /onboarding route

title: Search Page
description: The web UI must provide debounced full-text search across symbols, files, and requirements.
acceptance_criteria: Search accepts free-text queries with 300ms debounce;Minimum 2 characters required to trigger search;Results grouped by type showing symbols requirements and files;Each result shows title description and navigation link;Click navigates to detail page

title: Settings Page
description: The web UI must provide a settings page with dark/light theme toggle and API endpoint display.
acceptance_criteria: Dark and light theme toggle via useTheme hook;Theme stored in React context and applied via data-theme HTML attribute;Read-only display of NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL endpoint;Settings accessible at /settings route

title: Help Page
description: The web UI must provide a static help page covering all platform features and keyboard shortcuts.
acceptance_criteria: Getting Started section with initial setup guidance;Repositories section explaining indexing;Requirements and Traceability section;AI Features section covering discuss review explain;Admin and Configuration section;Keyboard Shortcuts reference

title: Admin Panel with Five Tabs
description: The web UI admin page must have tabs for System Status, LLM Configuration, Authentication, Repositories, and Knowledge.
acceptance_criteria: System Status tab fetches /api/v1/admin/status showing version uptime db and worker status;LLM Configuration tab shows current provider and model settings;Authentication tab shows auth mode and OIDC configuration;Repositories tab shows repo inventory;Knowledge tab fetches /api/v1/admin/knowledge showing per-repo artifact counts;Test Worker and Test LLM buttons

title: Traceability Matrix Export
description: The system must export the requirement-to-code traceability matrix as CSV or JSON via REST endpoints.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/v1/export/traceability with format parameter;GET /api/v1/export/requirements exports all requirements as CSV;GET /api/v1/export/symbols exports all symbols as CSV;CSV format compatible with import for round-trip;JSON format includes full metadata

title: Knowledge Engine CliffNotes
description: The system must generate CliffNotes documents summarizing a repository for a target audience at a specified depth.
acceptance_criteria: generateCliffNotes mutation accepts repositoryId audience and depth;Worker assembles repository snapshot JSON and calls LLM;Response includes sections with title content summary confidence and evidence;Required sections added as inferred stubs if LLM omits them;Knowledge artifact stored with sections and evidence in KnowledgeStore

title: Knowledge Engine Learning Path
description: The system must generate Learning Path documents providing step-by-step learning sequences for a repository.
acceptance_criteria: generateLearningPath mutation accepts repositoryId audience depth and optional focusArea;Worker generates ordered learning steps with objectives and estimated time;Each step references file paths and symbol IDs as evidence;Knowledge artifact stored with steps as sections

title: Knowledge Engine Code Tour
description: The system must generate Code Tour documents providing guided file-by-file walkthroughs of a repository.
acceptance_criteria: generateCodeTour mutation accepts repositoryId audience depth and optional theme;Worker generates ordered tour stops with title description file path and line range;Knowledge artifact stored with stops as sections

title: Knowledge Engine System Explanation
description: The system must answer freeform questions about a repository's architecture and design using LLM analysis.
acceptance_criteria: explainSystem mutation accepts repositoryId and question string;Worker assembles snapshot and calls LLM;Returns explanation as markdown text without storing an artifact;Response includes model and token usage

title: Knowledge Artifact Management
description: The system must store, retrieve, refresh, and export knowledge artifacts with sections and evidence.
acceptance_criteria: StoreKnowledgeArtifact creates artifact with type audience depth status and source revision;Artifacts marked stale when repository is reindexed;refreshKnowledgeArtifact regenerates by delegating to appropriate generator;DeleteKnowledgeArtifact cascades to sections and evidence;Export as JSON Markdown or HTML via GET /api/v1/export/knowledge/{id}

title: Knowledge Admin Endpoint
description: The admin API must expose a knowledge inventory endpoint showing per-repository artifact counts and status.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/v1/admin/knowledge returns per-repo artifact inventory;Shows count of artifacts by status (ready pending generating stale);Knowledge tab in admin panel displays this data

title: KnowledgeService gRPC Worker
description: The Python worker must register a KnowledgeService with four RPCs: GenerateCliffNotes, GenerateLearningPath, ExplainSystem, GenerateCodeTour.
acceptance_criteria: KnowledgeServicer registered on gRPC server in __main__.py;GenerateCliffNotes parses LLM JSON into sections with evidence;GenerateLearningPath parses steps with file and symbol references;ExplainSystem returns raw markdown explanation;GenerateCodeTour parses tour stops with file and line range

title: SSE Event Stream
description: The API must provide a Server-Sent Events endpoint for real-time notifications of repository, requirement, and link events.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/v1/events subscribes to all events via wildcard;Token passed as query parameter for EventSource compatibility;30-second heartbeat prevents connection timeout;Events include repo.index.started completed failed and requirement.imported linked;Events include link.verified link.rejected and review.completed

title: Toast Notification System
description: The web UI must display real-time toast notifications for server events using the SSE stream.
acceptance_criteria: Notifications component subscribes to useEventStream hook;Up to 5 toasts displayed simultaneously in bottom-right corner;Each toast auto-removes after 5000ms;Events translated to human-readable messages;Handles 8 event types from SSE stream

title: Command Palette
description: The web UI must provide a command palette accessible via Cmd+K with navigation shortcuts.
acceptance_criteria: Command palette opens on Cmd+K or Ctrl+K using cmdk library;Navigation commands include Dashboard Repositories Requirements Settings;Additional commands for Search Admin Help and Setup Wizard;Keyboard shortcuts displayed next to commands;Closes after selection or backdrop click

title: Dark and Light Theme Support
description: The web UI must support dark and light themes controlled by a ThemeProvider context.
acceptance_criteria: ThemeProvider sets data-theme attribute on document root;CSS variables scoped to data-theme dark and light selectors;Default theme is dark;Theme toggle on settings page;Theme persisted in React state

title: Code Viewer with Requirement Overlays
description: The web UI must render a read-only CodeMirror editor with colored line overlays indicating requirement coverage by category.
acceptance_criteria: CodeMirror 6 editor with oneDark theme;Language detection for go python typescript javascript java rust cpp;Overlays colored by category: business blue security red data green compliance purple performance yellow;Optional onLineClick handler for line selection;Streaming text component for AI output display

title: Traceability Matrix Visualization
description: The web UI must render an interactive traceability matrix showing requirements vs symbols with confidence indicators.
acceptance_criteria: Table with requirements as rows and symbols as columns;Colored circles indicate link confidence: green for verified blue for high yellow for medium grey for low;Green ring overlay for verified links;Sticky left column for requirement IDs;Optional onCellClick handler for cell selection

title: Dependency Graph Visualization
description: The web UI must render an interactive dependency graph showing module file symbol and requirement nodes.
acceptance_criteria: React Flow graph with four node types: module file symbol requirement;Nodes positioned in 4-column grid layout;Node border color varies by type;Animated edges show relationships;Includes background grid and zoom controls

title: Coverage Chart
description: The web UI must render a bar chart showing per-category requirement coverage percentages.
acceptance_criteria: Recharts BarChart with category-colored bars;Y-axis 0-100 percent;Tooltip shows coverage percentage;Category colors match requirement overlay palette;Configurable title prop

title: Empty State Components
description: The web UI must show guided empty states with action buttons when no repositories, requirements, or search results exist.
acceptance_criteria: NoRepositories shows folder icon with Add Repository action;NoRequirements shows file icon with Import action;NoResults shows search icon with Clear action;All use base EmptyState component with optional icon title description and action

title: Error Boundary
description: The web UI must wrap pages in an error boundary that catches render errors and provides recovery.
acceptance_criteria: React class component using getDerivedStateFromError;Error message displayed with Try Again button;Recovery resets error state to null;Wraps all authenticated page content

title: AI Concurrency Limiter
description: The API must limit concurrent AI GraphQL operations to a maximum of 5 and return 429 when exceeded.
acceptance_criteria: Semaphore-based limiter wraps GraphQL endpoint;Maximum 5 concurrent AI operations;Returns HTTP 429 Too Many Requests when limit exceeded;Non-AI GraphQL operations not affected

title: Security Headers Middleware
description: The API must set security headers on all responses including CSP, X-Frame-Options, and Referrer-Policy.
acceptance_criteria: X-Content-Type-Options nosniff;X-Frame-Options DENY;Referrer-Policy strict-origin-when-cross-origin;Permissions-Policy restricts camera microphone geolocation;Content-Security-Policy with default-src and script-src directives

title: OIDC Authentication Flow
description: The OSS API must support optional OIDC authentication with login redirect and callback endpoints.
acceptance_criteria: GET /auth/oidc/login redirects to OIDC authorization URL;GET /auth/oidc/callback exchanges code for JWT and sets session cookie;GET /auth/info returns oidc_enabled flag;OIDC configuration via config file with issuer_url client_id client_secret redirect_url and scopes

title: Auth Setup Flow
description: The API must support a first-time setup flow where the initial password is created via POST /auth/setup.
acceptance_criteria: POST /auth/setup creates initial password and returns JWT;Auto-login on successful setup with session cookie;Endpoint disabled after first password is set;GET /auth/info returns setup_done flag;Rate limited to 10 requests per minute

title: Password Change
description: The API must support password changes for authenticated users via POST /auth/change-password.
acceptance_criteria: POST /auth/change-password validates old password;Sets new password on successful validation;Requires JWT authentication;Rate limited with auth endpoints

title: API Token Management
description: The API must support creating, listing, and revoking API tokens for programmatic access.
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/v1/tokens creates a named token and returns full token string once;GET /api/v1/tokens lists all tokens showing prefix only;DELETE /api/v1/tokens/{id} revokes a token by ID;Tokens usable as Bearer auth alternative to JWT

title: Admin Status and Config API
description: The admin API must expose system status, configuration, and diagnostic endpoints.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/v1/admin/status returns version uptime db_status worker_status and knowledge stats;GET /api/v1/admin/config returns sanitized config with no secrets;PUT /api/v1/admin/config accepts config updates;POST /api/v1/admin/test-worker calls worker health check;POST /api/v1/admin/test-llm tests LLM availability

title: VS Code Extension Discuss Command
description: The VS Code extension must call the discussCode GraphQL mutation and display results in a webview panel.
acceptance_criteria: Command prompts for question via input box;Resolves repository ID from workspace;Sends full file or selection as context;Opens DiscussionPanel webview showing question and answer;Adds to discussion tree view history

title: VS Code Extension Review Command
description: The VS Code extension must call the reviewCode GraphQL mutation and display findings in a webview panel.
acceptance_criteria: Command prompts for template via quick pick;Templates: security solid performance reliability maintainability;Opens ReviewPanel webview showing score and findings;Findings color-coded by severity with line references

title: VS Code Extension Knowledge Commands
description: The VS Code extension must support four knowledge generation commands: cliffNotes, learningPath, codeTour, and explainSystem.
acceptance_criteria: generateCliffNotes command calls mutation and opens KnowledgePanel;generateLearningPath command calls mutation and opens KnowledgePanel;generateCodeTour command calls mutation and opens KnowledgePanel;explainSystem command prompts for question and opens ExplainPanel;All resolve repository ID and check server connectivity

title: VS Code CodeLens Provider
description: The VS Code extension must show CodeLens annotations above functions indicating linked requirements.
acceptance_criteria: CodeLens provider registered for 9 languages;Queries symbols for current file then requirement links per symbol;Shows requirement IDs as checklist icon with comma-separated list;Clicking CodeLens triggers showRequirements command

title: VS Code Hover Provider
description: The VS Code extension must show linked requirements and signatures on hover over indexed symbols.
acceptance_criteria: Hover provider registered for 9 languages;Resolves symbol at cursor position by line range;Shows symbol name signature and doc comment;Shows linked requirements with confidence and verified status

title: VS Code Gutter Decorations
description: The VS Code extension must show colored background decorations on lines with linked requirements grouped by confidence.
acceptance_criteria: Four decoration types for VERIFIED HIGH MEDIUM and LOW confidence;Highest confidence wins when multiple links cover same symbol;Colors applied as full-line background and overview ruler;Updates on active editor change and document change;Disposed on extension deactivation

title: VS Code Requirements Tree View
description: The VS Code extension must provide a sidebar tree view showing requirements for the first repository.
acceptance_criteria: Tree provider fetches repositories and takes first repo;Queries up to 100 requirements;Shows externalId as label and title as description;Refresh method fires event emitter;Placeholder shown when no requirements exist

title: VS Code Discussion Tree View
description: The VS Code extension must provide a sidebar tree view showing discussion history from the current session.
acceptance_criteria: In-memory discussion list with addDiscussion method;Shows questions truncated to 50 chars as labels;Shows answers truncated to 80 chars as descriptions;Placeholder when no discussions yet;Most recent discussion appears first

title: VS Code Knowledge Tree View
description: The VS Code extension must provide a two-level sidebar tree view showing knowledge artifacts and their sections.
acceptance_criteria: Top level shows artifacts with type label and section count;Warning icon shown for stale artifacts;Expanding artifact shows sections ordered by orderIndex;Each section shows confidence percentage and inferred badge;Placeholder when no artifacts exist

title: VS Code Webview Panels
description: The VS Code extension must render HTML webview panels for discussion, review, knowledge, and explanation results.
acceptance_criteria: DiscussionPanel shows question block and answer with optional references;ReviewPanel shows template name score percentage and severity-colored findings;KnowledgePanel shows metadata sections with confidence and evidence;ExplainPanel shows question and explanation in pre-wrap;All panels use enableScripts false and HTML-escaped content

title: VS Code Configure Command
description: The VS Code extension must provide a configure command to set API URL and authentication token.
acceptance_criteria: Command prompts for API URL with current value shown;Prompts for token as password input;Saves both to workspace configuration;Tests connection and shows success or warning message

title: VS Code Repository Resolution
description: The VS Code extension must resolve the active workspace to a server-side repository by name matching.
acceptance_criteria: Fetches all repositories from server;Matches by workspace folder name case-insensitive;Falls back to auto-select when only one repo exists;Shows QuickPick when multiple repos match;Error classified for LLM unavailability and GraphQL errors

title: gRPC Worker Service Registration
description: The Python worker must register ReasoningService, LinkingService, RequirementsService, and KnowledgeService on the gRPC server.
acceptance_criteria: All four servicers registered in __main__.py;gRPC health servicer marks all four services as SERVING;gRPC server reflection enabled for all service descriptors;50 MB send and receive message limits;Graceful shutdown on SIGINT and SIGTERM with 5 second grace period

title: LLM Provider Factory
description: The worker must support configurable LLM providers via factory pattern supporting Anthropic, OpenAI, Ollama, and vLLM.
acceptance_criteria: create_llm_provider dispatches on provider name;AnthropicProvider uses anthropic AsyncAnthropic with NOT_GIVEN for optional system;OpenAICompatProvider works with any chat completions endpoint;Default base URLs for Ollama and vLLM;FakeLLMProvider returns deterministic fixture responses in test mode

title: LLM Router with Fallback
description: The worker must support an LLM router that tries multiple providers in order with automatic fallback.
acceptance_criteria: LLMRouter wraps list of LLMProvider instances;Complete method tries each provider in order;On exception logs warning and tries next provider;Raises RuntimeError if all providers fail;Stream method uses same fallback logic

title: Summary Cache
description: The worker must cache summaries and reviews in an in-memory LRU cache keyed by content hash and model version.
acceptance_criteria: SummaryCache uses OrderedDict with threading Lock;Max size configurable defaulting to 1000;Summaries cached without TTL;Reviews cached with 7-day TTL;Invalidate removes all entries matching a content hash

title: Circuit Breaker
description: The worker must implement a circuit breaker for LLM calls that opens after repeated failures.
acceptance_criteria: CircuitBreaker opens after failure_threshold consecutive failures;is_open returns true when circuit is open;Auto-resets to closed after reset_timeout seconds;record_success zeroes failure counter and closes circuit;Default threshold 3 failures and 60 second reset

title: Usage Tracker
description: The worker must track LLM API usage records for cost attribution and monitoring.
acceptance_criteria: UsageTracker collects LLMUsageRecord objects;record method appends to thread-safe list;records property returns a copy;clear method empties the list;Records include provider model input_tokens output_tokens operation

title: Embedding Provider Implementation
description: The worker must support embedding generation via Ollama native API and OpenAI-compatible endpoints.
acceptance_criteria: OllamaEmbeddingProvider uses native /api/embed endpoint;OpenAICompatEmbeddingProvider uses /v1/embeddings endpoint;FakeEmbeddingProvider returns deterministic SHA-256-based vectors;Batch embedding support for multiple texts;Configurable dimension model and timeout

title: SurrealDB Python Client
description: The Python worker must have a working SurrealDB HTTP client for querying symbol and requirement data.
acceptance_criteria: SurrealClient converts ws URL to http and strips /rpc suffix;connect method creates httpx client with basic auth and verifies via GET /health;query method POSTs to /sql endpoint;connected property tracks connection state;close method disposes httpx client

title: Go gRPC Client for Worker
description: The Go API server must have a gRPC client to call the Python worker ReasoningService, LinkingService, RequirementsService, and KnowledgeService.
acceptance_criteria: Client connects to worker at configured address;Timeout and retry configuration;Health check before first use;Client used by GraphQL resolvers for AI operations;Supports all 16 RPCs across 4 services

title: Redis Cache Implementation
description: The Redis cache client must use go-redis for real caching instead of falling back to in-memory.
acceptance_criteria: go-redis client connects to configured Redis URL;Cache operations support get set and delete with TTL;Graceful fallback to in-memory when Redis unavailable;Cache keys prefixed by tenant ID

title: Prometheus Metrics Instrumentation
description: The API server must expose real Prometheus metrics for HTTP requests, GraphQL operations, worker RPCs, and indexing.
acceptance_criteria: httpRequestsTotal counter by method and path;httpRequestDuration histogram;gqlOperationsTotal counter;workerRPCCalls and workerRPCErrors counters;indexingTotal counter;Metrics served at GET /metrics in Prometheus text format

title: Kubernetes Health Probes
description: The readiness probe must verify actual backend connectivity not just return a static response.
acceptance_criteria: Readyz checks SurrealDB ping and returns per-component status;Readyz checks worker gRPC health if configured;Healthz returns 200 with ok text if process is alive;JSON response with api database and worker status fields

title: Metrics Middleware
description: The API must track HTTP request counts and durations via middleware on all routes.
acceptance_criteria: metricsMiddleware increments request counter per request;Accumulates duration in atomic float64;graphqlCountMiddleware increments GraphQL operation counter;Middleware applied to all routes before handlers

title: Docker Compose Full Stack
description: The system must be deployable via docker compose up with web UI, API, worker, and SurrealDB.
acceptance_criteria: Single docker-compose.yml starts all services;Web UI accessible on port 3000;API accessible on port 8080;Worker connects to API via gRPC;SurrealDB data persisted in named volume

title: Kubernetes Deployment
description: The system must deploy to Kubernetes with separate deployments for API, web, worker, and SurrealDB.
acceptance_criteria: Deployment manifests for all four components;Services and ingress configured for external access;ConfigMaps for non-secret configuration;Secrets for credentials and API keys;PersistentVolumeClaims for SurrealDB data

title: Tekton CI/CD Pipeline
description: The Tekton pipeline must build all container images and deploy them to the cluster.
acceptance_criteria: Clone task fetches repository;Kaniko builds API web and worker images sequentially due to RWO PVC;Images pushed to internal registry at 192.168.10.222:30500;Rollout restart triggers deployment update;Telegram notification on pipeline completion via finally block

title: Container Image Tagging
description: All container images must be tagged with the git commit SHA from the clone task.
acceptance_criteria: Image tag format is sha-COMMITSHA from tasks.clone.results.COMMIT_SHA;Images pushed to registry with SHA tag;Deployment manifests reference SHA tags;Pipeline workspace uses volumeClaimTemplate

title: Admin Auth Persistence
description: Admin password and authentication state must persist to SurrealDB across pod restarts.
acceptance_criteria: Admin user record stored in SurrealDB;Password hashed with bcrypt;Login validates against persisted credentials;Password change updates persisted record

title: VS Code Repository Mapping
description: The VS Code extension must map local workspace folders to server-side repositories by name matching.
acceptance_criteria: Extension resolves workspace folder name to repository;Case-insensitive name matching against server repositories;Falls back to single-repo auto-select;QuickPick shown when ambiguous;Repository ID used for all subsequent API calls

title: Tekton Webhook Configuration
description: The Tekton webhook trigger must be accessible from GitHub for automated pipeline runs on push.
acceptance_criteria: EventListener service exposed via ingress or NodePort;GitHub webhook configured with cluster-external URL;Push events trigger pipeline runs automatically;Webhook secret validates payload authenticity

title: Embedding Configuration
description: Worker embedding configuration must be provided via environment variables with SOURCEBRIDGE_WORKER prefix.
acceptance_criteria: embedding_provider configurable as ollama or openai-compatible;embedding_model defaults to nomic-embed-text;embedding_dimension defaults to 768;embedding_base_url configurable;embedding_api_key optional for Ollama

title: Requirement Enrichment via LLM
description: The worker must support enriching imported requirements with LLM-suggested priority and tags.
acceptance_criteria: EnrichRequirement RPC accepts a requirement and returns enhanced copy;LLM generates suggested_priority and suggested_tags as JSON;Suggested tags merged with existing tags and deduplicated;Results stored alongside original requirement via enrichRequirement mutation

title: Code Graph Visualization
description: The web UI must render interactive code dependency graphs using React Flow.
acceptance_criteria: Graph shows nodes for symbols and edges for relationships;Four node types with type-specific border colors;Zoom pan and click-to-navigate supported;Animated edges show relationship direction;Graph accessible from repository detail page

title: Module-Level Data Model
description: The system must extract and store module-level data from directory structure with contained files and symbols.
acceptance_criteria: Modules identified from directory structure;GetModules returns all modules for a repository;Modules linked to contained files and key symbols;Module summaries generated via summarize_module

title: Call Graph Storage
description: The system must store caller-callee relationships between symbols for dependency analysis.
acceptance_criteria: GetCallers returns symbol IDs that call a given symbol;GetCallees returns symbol IDs called by a given symbol;Call graph stored during indexing phase;Relationships derived from AST analysis

title: Import Tracking
description: The system must store import relationships between files for dependency analysis.
acceptance_criteria: GetImports returns all import records for a repository;Import relationships stored during indexing;Used for dependency graph visualization;File-to-file import edges tracked

title: LLM Usage Tracking
description: The system must record and expose LLM API usage data for cost monitoring.
acceptance_criteria: StoreLLMUsage records provider model operation and token counts;GetLLMUsage returns records in reverse chronological order;LLM usage query exposed via GraphQL;Dashboard shows recent AI operations from usage data;Token totals shown in platform stats

title: Embedding Cache
description: The system must cache computed embedding vectors for symbols and requirements to avoid recomputation.
acceptance_criteria: StoreEmbedding caches vector for a target ID;GetEmbedding retrieves cached vector;Target can be symbol requirement or file;Cache shared between linking and search operations

title: Review Results Storage
description: The system must persist structured code review results for retrieval and display.
acceptance_criteria: StoreReviewResult saves full review with findings and score;GetReviewResults returns all reviews for a target;Review results include template findings and score;Results retrievable via GraphQL

title: Tenant-Filtered Store
description: The system must support wrapping the GraphStore with a tenant filter that restricts data access to allowed repository IDs.
acceptance_criteria: TenantFilteredStore wraps GraphStore with allowed repo ID set;ListRepositories filtered to allowed IDs only;GetRepository returns error if not in allowed set;All downstream queries scoped through filtered store;Injected into request context by RepoAccessMiddleware

title: Repository Access Middleware
description: The API must inject a tenant-filtered store into the request context for non-default tenants.
acceptance_criteria: RepoAccessMiddleware reads tenant from context;For non-default tenants looks up allowed repo IDs via RepoAccessChecker;Wraps store in TenantFilteredStore;StoreFromContext retrieves per-request store;Default tenant gets unfiltered access

title: Incremental Re-indexing
description: Repository re-indexing must be incremental using content hash comparison to skip unchanged files.
acceptance_criteria: reindexRepository checks previous content hashes;Only changed files re-indexed;git pull run for remote repos before comparison;Knowledge artifacts marked stale on reindex;Returns updated repository with new lastIndexedAt

title: Repository Error State
description: The system must track repository error states when indexing or cloning fails.
acceptance_criteria: SetRepositoryError marks repo status as error with message;Error state visible in repository list;Error cleared on successful reindex;Error message stored alongside repository record

title: Platform Stats Query
description: The GraphQL API must expose aggregate platform statistics for the dashboard.
acceptance_criteria: platformStats returns counts of repos files symbols requirements links and imports;Includes totalInputTokens and totalOutputTokens from LLM usage;Used by dashboard for stat cards;Delegated to store Stats method

title: Version Query
description: The GraphQL API must expose the application version, commit SHA, and build date.
acceptance_criteria: version query returns version commitSHA and buildDate;Values set at build time via linker flags;Displayed in admin status panel

title: Health Query
description: The GraphQL API must expose a health query with per-service status.
acceptance_criteria: health query returns overall status and services array;Each service has name and status fields;Used by VS Code extension for server connectivity check

title: Feature Flags Query
description: The GraphQL features query must return 21 boolean flags covering enterprise, knowledge engine, and custom template capabilities.
acceptance_criteria: 10 enterprise flags: multiTenant sso linearConnector jiraConnector githubApp gitlabApp auditLog webhooks customTemplates billing;4 OSS knowledge flags: cliffNotes learningPaths codeTours systemExplain;7 enterprise knowledge flags: multiAudienceKnowledge customKnowledgeTemplates advancedLearningPaths slideGeneration podcastGeneration knowledgeScheduling knowledgeExport;Flags computed from edition env var and service availability

title: Entitlements Checker
description: The system must define feature-to-plan mappings for gating capabilities by subscription tier.
acceptance_criteria: featurePlans maps feature names to minimum required plan;team plan features include sso connectors webhooks and multi_audience_knowledge;enterprise plan features include audit_log custom_templates helm and knowledge_scheduling;IsAllowed returns Check with Allowed bool and RequiredPlan;Features not in map are always allowed

title: CLI Serve Command
description: The CLI must support a sourcebridge serve command that starts the HTTP API server.
acceptance_criteria: Command starts HTTP server on configured port;Connects to SurrealDB and initializes stores;Initializes worker gRPC client;Sets up JWT auth and optional OIDC;Supports --port flag override

title: CLI Index Command
description: The CLI must support a sourcebridge index command that indexes a local repository.
acceptance_criteria: Command accepts repository path as argument;Indexing extracts symbols and relationships;Prints file symbol module and relation counts;Supports --json and --retry flags

title: CLI Ask Command
description: The CLI must support a sourcebridge ask command that forwards a question to the Python worker.
acceptance_criteria: Command accepts question as argument;Delegates to uv run python cli_ask.py;Supports --repo and --json flags;Returns AI answer text

title: CLI Review Command
description: The CLI must support a sourcebridge review command that generates structured code reviews.
acceptance_criteria: Command accepts file or directory path;Walks source files and forwards each to worker;Supports --template flag for review type;Supports --repo and --json flags;Exit code reflects review severity

title: CLI Trace Command
description: The CLI must support a sourcebridge trace command that finds requirement-to-code links.
acceptance_criteria: Command accepts requirement ID as argument;Indexes repo and optionally imports requirements file;Searches symbol doc comments for requirement ID references;Displays found links;Supports --repo --requirements --json --min-confidence flags

title: CLI Import Command
description: The CLI must support a sourcebridge import command that parses markdown or CSV requirements.
acceptance_criteria: Command accepts file path as argument;Auto-detects format from file extension;Parses requirements and stores in graph store;Supports --repo-id --format --json flags

title: CLI Config Commands
description: The CLI must support sourcebridge config get, list, and validate subcommands.
acceptance_criteria: config get loads and prints full config as JSON;config list is identical to get;config validate loads config and runs Validate method;Non-zero exit code on validation failure

title: Kubernetes Secrets Management
description: Kubernetes secrets must not contain placeholder values in committed manifests.
acceptance_criteria: Secret templates use documented placeholder format;Real values injected at deploy time or via sealed secrets;CHANGE_ME values never deployed to cluster;Secret rotation documented in runbook

title: GraphQL Playground Security
description: The GraphQL playground must only be available in development mode.
acceptance_criteria: Playground mounted at GET /api/v1/playground only when cfg.IsDevelopment is true;Not accessible in production deployments;CSP headers prevent embedding

title: Configurable Linking Thresholds
description: The system must support configurable confidence thresholds for different display contexts.
acceptance_criteria: min_confidence_ui defaults to 0.5 for web display;min_confidence_codelens defaults to 0.7 for VS Code CodeLens;min_confidence_pr_comment defaults to 0.8 for PR comments;Thresholds configurable via config file

title: Worker Test Mode
description: The worker must support a test_mode flag that replaces LLM and embedding providers with deterministic fakes.
acceptance_criteria: SOURCEBRIDGE_WORKER_TEST_MODE=true activates fake providers;FakeLLMProvider returns fixture-backed responses keyed by prompt keywords;FakeEmbeddingProvider returns SHA-256-based deterministic vectors;All automated tests run against fakes for reproducibility
